events.description: On January 12, 2009, Satoshi Nakamoto sent 10 BTC to Hal Finney in Bitcoin's first recorded peer-to-peer transaction, proving the network worked beyond mining rewards.
events.title: From Satoshi to Hal

events.description: This transaction is associated with the famous Bitcoin Pizza Day purchase in which Laszlo Hanyecz spent 10,000 BTC for two pizzas on May 22, 2010.
events.title: Pizza Purchase
Len Sassaman Tribute
events.description: According to Wikipedia, 'An ASCII art tribute by Dan Kaminsky in honor of Len, to be permanently embedded into Bitcoin's blockchain, was first announced during "The Wake for Len Sassaman" held at the DNA Lounge in San Francisco on July 30, 2011. Kaminsky revealed it publicly during Black Hat USA 2011 at the Caesars Palace venue in Las Vegas.'

events.title: Lowest Block Hash (So Far)
events.description: Mined by Binance on June 15, 2020, block 634,842 has the lowest hash of any block in bitcoin. That is at least at the time of this writing (March 27th 2026).
